description_long: This object is a heavy-duty hammer with a robust, solid metal head and a short cylindrical handle. The head is rectangular with a flat striking face and a pointed horn on one end, resembling a blacksmith's anvil rather than a typical hammer. The handle appears to be made of metal and is inserted into the top of the head at an angle. The surface of the head is dark, likely forged steel, with a matte, slightly rough finish and some visible signs of rust or oxidation. The base is wide and stable, designed to absorb impact and provide a firm foundation during use. The overall appearance suggests it is intended for industrial or blacksmithing tasks in urban workshops or garages.
description: A heavy, industrial hammer with a solid metal head, flat striking face, and a short, angled metal handle. The head is dark, robust, and shows signs of wear and rust.
description_view_0: Front view shows the wide, flat base and the angled metal handle protruding from the top, with the pointed horn barely visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the rectangular head, flat striking face, and the pointed horn extending from the opposite end, with the handle clearly visible on top.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the broad base and the back of the head, with the handle extending at an angle and the horn less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the full length of the pointed horn, the rectangular head, and the handle's position on top.
attribute_handle_type: short, straight, cylindrical metal handle with a smooth surface and no visible grip enhancements
attribute_head_type: rectangular anvil-style head with a flat striking face and a pointed horn, resembling a blacksmith's anvil rather than a typical hammer head
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible rust and oxidation on the head and handle, with some surface wear
